---
title: "Univariate Analysis"
subtitle: "Exploring the shape and spread of individual variables — one at a time"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib
sys.path.insert(0, str(pathlib.Path(".").resolve()))
import utils
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import seaborn as sns
from scipy import stats

df = utils.load_data()
utils.apply_dark_theme()
```

Univariate analysis examines **one variable at a time**, giving us a clear picture of its central tendency, spread, and shape before we consider relationships with other variables. For each key metric we report:

- **Mean** — the arithmetic average, sensitive to outliers
- **Median** — the middle value, robust to extremes
- **Mode** — the most common value
- **Std Dev / IQR** — two complementary measures of spread

---

## Match Duration

### Summary Statistics

```{python}
#| label: tbl-dur-stats

dur = df.groupby(["Match", "team_clean"])["dur_min"].first().drop_duplicates()

stats_dur = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Mean", "Median", "Mode", "Std Dev", "Min", "Max",
               "Q1 (25th %ile)", "Q3 (75th %ile)", "IQR"],
    "Value": [
        f"{dur.mean():.2f} min",
        f"{dur.median():.2f} min",
        f"{dur.mode()[0]:.2f} min",
        f"{dur.std():.2f} min",
        f"{dur.min():.2f} min",
        f"{dur.max():.2f} min",
        f"{dur.quantile(0.25):.2f} min",
        f"{dur.quantile(0.75):.2f} min",
        f"{dur.quantile(0.75) - dur.quantile(0.25):.2f} min",
    ]
})
display(stats_dur.style.hide(axis="index"))
```

### Distribution

```{python}
#| label: fig-dur-dist
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of match durations (one point per game). The KDE overlay shows the smooth density."

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

# Histogram + KDE
ax = axes[0]
sns.histplot(
    dur, bins=15, kde=True,
    color=utils.PALETTE["primary"],
    edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"],
    line_kws={"lw": 2.5, "color": utils.PALETTE["secondary"]},
    ax=ax,
)
ax.axvline(dur.mean(),   color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], lw=1.8,
           linestyle="--", label=f"Mean: {dur.mean():.1f} min")
ax.axvline(dur.median(), color=utils.PALETTE["success"], lw=1.8,
           linestyle=":",  label=f"Median: {dur.median():.1f} min")
ax.legend(fontsize=9)
ax.set_xlabel("Duration (minutes)")
ax.set_ylabel("Count")
ax.set_title("Histogram + KDE")

# Box plot
ax2 = axes[1]
bp = ax2.boxplot(
    dur.dropna(),
    vert=True,
    patch_artist=True,
    widths=0.4,
    boxprops=dict(facecolor=utils.PALETTE["primary"], color=utils.PALETTE["secondary"]),
    medianprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], lw=2.5),
    whiskerprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["muted"], lw=1.5),
    capprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["muted"], lw=1.5),
    flierprops=dict(marker="o", color=utils.PALETTE["danger"],
                    markerfacecolor=utils.PALETTE["danger"], markersize=5),
)
ax2.set_ylabel("Duration (minutes)")
ax2.set_title("Box Plot — Spread & Outliers")
ax2.set_xticks([])

fig.suptitle("Match Duration Distribution", fontsize=14, y=1.02)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** Match durations cluster tightly around **22–26 minutes**, a hallmark of high-level MPL play where teams snowball aggressively. The box plot shows little spread and minimal outliers, confirming games rarely drag past 30 minutes in this season.
:::

---

## Kills

### Summary Statistics

```{python}
#| label: tbl-kills-stats

kills = df["K"]

stats_k = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Mean", "Median", "Mode", "Std Dev", "Min", "Max",
               "Q1", "Q3", "IQR", "Skewness"],
    "Value": [
        f"{kills.mean():.2f}",
        f"{kills.median():.2f}",
        f"{int(kills.mode()[0])}",
        f"{kills.std():.2f}",
        f"{kills.min()}",
        f"{kills.max()}",
        f"{kills.quantile(0.25):.1f}",
        f"{kills.quantile(0.75):.1f}",
        f"{kills.quantile(0.75) - kills.quantile(0.25):.1f}",
        f"{kills.skew():.2f}",
    ]
})
display(stats_k.style.hide(axis="index"))
```

### Distribution

```{python}
#| label: fig-kills-dist
#| fig-cap: "Kill count distribution across all player-game rows."

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

ax = axes[0]
counts = kills.value_counts().sort_index()
ax.bar(
    counts.index, counts.values,
    color=utils.PALETTE["danger"],
    edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"],
    alpha=0.9,
)
ax.axvline(kills.mean(),   color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], lw=2, ls="--",
           label=f"Mean {kills.mean():.1f}")
ax.axvline(kills.median(), color=utils.PALETTE["success"], lw=2, ls=":",
           label=f"Median {kills.median():.0f}")
ax.set_xlabel("Kills")
ax.set_ylabel("Frequency")
ax.set_title("Kill Count — Frequency Distribution")
ax.legend(fontsize=9)

# Violin plot
ax2 = axes[1]
parts = ax2.violinplot(
    kills.dropna(), vert=True,
    showmeans=True, showmedians=True,
)
for pc in parts["bodies"]:
    pc.set_facecolor(utils.PALETTE["danger"])
    pc.set_alpha(0.7)
for key in ["cmeans", "cmedians", "cbars", "cmins", "cmaxes"]:
    if key in parts:
        parts[key].set_color(utils.PALETTE["warning"] if key == "cmeans"
                             else utils.PALETTE["success"] if key == "cmedians"
                             else utils.PALETTE["muted"])
ax2.set_xticks([])
ax2.set_ylabel("Kills")
ax2.set_title("Violin Plot — Full Distribution Shape")

fig.suptitle("Kills per Player per Game", fontsize=14, y=1.02)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** Kill counts are **right-skewed** with mode at 0–2 — most players are not primary damage dealers. The long right tail represents carry heroes (Marksmen, Fighters) who can accumulate 10+ kills in a dominant game. The mean is pulled above the median by these high-kill outliers.
:::

---

## Deaths

```{python}
#| label: fig-deaths-dist
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of deaths per player per game."

deaths = df["D"]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

ax = axes[0]
dcounts = deaths.value_counts().sort_index()
ax.bar(
    dcounts.index, dcounts.values,
    color=utils.PALETTE["warning"],
    edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"],
    alpha=0.9,
)
ax.axvline(deaths.mean(), color=utils.PALETTE["danger"], lw=2, ls="--",
           label=f"Mean {deaths.mean():.1f}")
ax.set_xlabel("Deaths")
ax.set_ylabel("Frequency")
ax.set_title("Death Count — Frequency Distribution")
ax.legend(fontsize=9)

ax2 = axes[1]
ax2.boxplot(
    deaths.dropna(), vert=True,
    patch_artist=True, widths=0.4,
    boxprops=dict(facecolor=utils.PALETTE["warning"], color=utils.PALETTE["secondary"]),
    medianprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["danger"], lw=2.5),
    whiskerprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["muted"], lw=1.5),
    capprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["muted"]),
    flierprops=dict(marker="o", color=utils.PALETTE["danger"],
                    markerfacecolor=utils.PALETTE["danger"], markersize=4),
)
ax2.set_ylabel("Deaths")
ax2.set_xticks([])
ax2.set_title("Box Plot — Spread & Outliers")

fig.suptitle("Deaths per Player per Game", fontsize=14, y=1.02)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-deaths-stats
#| echo: false
pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Mean", "Median", "Mode", "Std Dev", "Q1", "Q3", "IQR", "Max"],
    "Value": [
        f"{deaths.mean():.2f}", f"{deaths.median():.1f}",
        f"{int(deaths.mode()[0])}",
        f"{deaths.std():.2f}",
        f"{deaths.quantile(0.25):.1f}", f"{deaths.quantile(0.75):.1f}",
        f"{deaths.quantile(0.75) - deaths.quantile(0.25):.1f}",
        f"{deaths.max()}",
    ]
}).style.hide(axis="index")
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** Deaths are distributed more symmetrically than kills, with a mode around 2–4. High death counts (6+) are relatively rare and typically signal a bad game for a carry player. Support and Tank players accept a slightly higher death count as part of frontline initiation.
:::

---

## Assists

```{python}
#| label: fig-assists-dist
#| fig-cap: "Assist count distribution — Supports and Tanks typically dominate this metric."

assists = df["A"]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

ax = axes[0]
sns.histplot(assists, bins=20, ax=ax,
             color=utils.PALETTE["success"], edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], kde=True,
             line_kws={"lw": 2, "color": utils.PALETTE["secondary"]})
ax.axvline(assists.mean(),   color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], lw=2, ls="--",
           label=f"Mean {assists.mean():.1f}")
ax.axvline(assists.median(), color=utils.PALETTE["accent"],  lw=2, ls=":",
           label=f"Median {assists.median():.0f}")
ax.set_xlabel("Assists")
ax.set_title("Histogram + KDE")
ax.legend(fontsize=9)

ax2 = axes[1]
ax2.boxplot(
    assists.dropna(), vert=True, patch_artist=True, widths=0.4,
    boxprops=dict(facecolor=utils.PALETTE["success"], color=utils.PALETTE["secondary"]),
    medianprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], lw=2.5),
    whiskerprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["muted"], lw=1.5),
    capprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["muted"]),
    flierprops=dict(marker="o", color=utils.PALETTE["danger"],
                    markerfacecolor=utils.PALETTE["danger"], markersize=4),
)
ax2.set_ylabel("Assists")
ax2.set_xticks([])
ax2.set_title("Box Plot")

fig.suptitle("Assists per Player per Game", fontsize=14, y=1.02)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** Assists are more broadly distributed than kills. Support players routinely record 10+ assists per game without a single kill, reflecting their team-enabling role. The right tail of the distribution highlights particularly active support/roam players in high-teamfight games.
:::

---

## Gold Earned

```{python}
#| label: fig-gold-dist
#| fig-cap: "Gold earned per player per game — the strongest proxy for resource efficiency."

gold = df["Gold"]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

ax = axes[0]
sns.histplot(gold, bins=25, ax=ax,
             color=utils.PALETTE["accent"], edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], kde=True,
             line_kws={"lw": 2, "color": utils.PALETTE["secondary"]})
ax.axvline(gold.mean(),   color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], lw=2, ls="--",
           label=f"Mean {gold.mean():,.0f}")
ax.axvline(gold.median(), color=utils.PALETTE["success"], lw=2, ls=":",
           label=f"Median {gold.median():,.0f}")
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x/1000:.0f}k"))
ax.set_xlabel("Gold Earned (thousands)")
ax.set_title("Histogram + KDE")
ax.legend(fontsize=9)

ax2 = axes[1]
parts = ax2.violinplot(gold.dropna(), vert=True, showmeans=True, showmedians=True)
for pc in parts["bodies"]:
    pc.set_facecolor(utils.PALETTE["accent"])
    pc.set_alpha(0.75)
parts["cmeans"].set_color(utils.PALETTE["warning"])
parts["cmedians"].set_color(utils.PALETTE["success"])
ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda y, _: f"{y/1000:.0f}k"))
ax2.set_xticks([])
ax2.set_ylabel("Gold (thousands)")
ax2.set_title("Violin Plot")

fig.suptitle("Gold Earned per Player per Game", fontsize=14, y=1.02)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-gold-stats
pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Mean", "Median", "Std Dev", "Min", "Max", "Q1", "Q3", "IQR"],
    "Value": [
        f"{gold.mean():,.0f}", f"{gold.median():,.0f}",
        f"{gold.std():,.0f}",
        f"{gold.min():,}", f"{gold.max():,}",
        f"{gold.quantile(0.25):,.0f}", f"{gold.quantile(0.75):,.0f}",
        f"{gold.quantile(0.75) - gold.quantile(0.25):,.0f}",
    ]
}).style.hide(axis="index")
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** Gold is roughly bell-shaped with a slight right skew. The **IQR of ~4,000–5,000 gold** covers the bulk of players, while top-lane fighters and gold-lane marksmen drive the high end. Low gold values belong primarily to support/roam players who sacrifice income for vision and utility.
:::

---

## KDA (Kill–Death–Assist Ratio)

```{python}
#| label: fig-kda-dist
#| fig-cap: "KDA = (Kills + Assists) / max(Deaths, 1). Capped at 20 for readability."

kda = df["kda"].clip(upper=20)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

ax = axes[0]
sns.histplot(kda, bins=30, ax=ax,
             color=utils.PALETTE["secondary"], edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], kde=True,
             line_kws={"lw": 2, "color": utils.PALETTE["primary"]})
ax.axvline(kda.mean(),   color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], lw=2, ls="--",
           label=f"Mean {kda.mean():.2f}")
ax.axvline(kda.median(), color=utils.PALETTE["success"], lw=2, ls=":",
           label=f"Median {kda.median():.2f}")
ax.set_xlabel("KDA (capped at 20)")
ax.set_title("Histogram + KDE")
ax.legend(fontsize=9)

ax2 = axes[1]
ax2.boxplot(
    kda.dropna(), vert=True, patch_artist=True, widths=0.4,
    boxprops=dict(facecolor=utils.PALETTE["secondary"], color=utils.PALETTE["primary"]),
    medianprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], lw=2.5),
    whiskerprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["muted"], lw=1.5),
    capprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["muted"]),
    flierprops=dict(marker="o", color=utils.PALETTE["danger"],
                    markerfacecolor=utils.PALETTE["danger"], markersize=4),
)
ax2.set_ylabel("KDA")
ax2.set_xticks([])
ax2.set_title("Box Plot")

fig.suptitle("KDA Distribution (all players)", fontsize=14, y=1.02)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** The KDA distribution is strongly right-skewed. A large fraction of players fall between KDA 2–5, representing solid competitive performances. The long right tail consists mainly of support heroes who die rarely but collect many assists — inflating the ratio — and hyper-carries who dominate without dying.
:::

---

## Role Distribution

```{python}
#| label: fig-role-dist
#| fig-cap: "How frequently each primary role was picked across all games."

role_counts = df["role"].value_counts().reset_index()
role_counts.columns = ["Role", "Count"]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5))

# Bar chart
ax = axes[0]
colors = [utils.ROLE_COLORS.get(r, utils.PALETTE["muted"]) for r in role_counts["Role"]]
bars = ax.bar(role_counts["Role"], role_counts["Count"],
              color=colors, edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], alpha=0.9)
for bar, cnt in zip(bars, role_counts["Count"]):
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, bar.get_height() + 3,
            str(cnt), ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=9,
            color=utils.PALETTE["text"], fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Primary Role")
ax.set_ylabel("Pick Count")
ax.set_title("Role Pick Frequency")
ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=30)

# Pie chart
ax2 = axes[1]
wedges, texts, autotexts = ax2.pie(
    role_counts["Count"],
    labels=role_counts["Role"],
    colors=colors,
    autopct="%1.1f%%",
    startangle=140,
    wedgeprops=dict(edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], linewidth=1.5),
    textprops=dict(color=utils.PALETTE["text"], fontsize=9),
)
for at in autotexts:
    at.set_color(utils.PALETTE["surface"])
    at.set_fontsize(8)
ax2.set_title("Role Share (Pie)", pad=12)

fig.suptitle("Role Pick Distribution — MPL Cambodia S6", fontsize=14, y=1.02)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** In every game, each team fields exactly one player per lane — typically Tank, Fighter, Marksman, Mage, and Support/Roam. The near-equal distribution across Fighter, Tank, Support, Marksman, and Mage confirms this standard 5-role meta, with Assassins appearing occasionally as substitutes for Fighter or Mage picks.
:::

---

## Objectives (Tower, Lord, Turtle)

```{python}
#| label: fig-objectives
#| fig-cap: "Per-game team objective counts — one data point per team per game."

ml = utils.match_level(df)
obj_cols = ["Tower", "Lord", "Turtle"]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 5))
obj_colors = [utils.PALETTE["accent"], utils.PALETTE["warning"], utils.PALETTE["success"]]

for ax, col, color in zip(axes, obj_cols, obj_colors):
    data = ml[col].dropna()
    counts = data.value_counts().sort_index()
    ax.bar(counts.index, counts.values, color=color, edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], alpha=0.9)
    ax.axvline(data.mean(), color=utils.PALETTE["text"], lw=1.8, ls="--",
               label=f"Mean: {data.mean():.1f}")
    ax.set_xlabel(col + "s")
    ax.set_ylabel("Frequency")
    ax.set_title(f"{col} Count Distribution")
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)

fig.suptitle("Team Objective Counts per Game", fontsize=14, y=1.02)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-objectives
obj_stats = ml[["Tower", "Lord", "Turtle"]].describe().T.round(2)
obj_stats.columns = ["Count", "Mean", "Std Dev", "Min", "Q1", "Median", "Q3", "Max"]
obj_stats.style
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** The **Tower** count varies widely (0 to 10+) because the winning team often demolishes everything while the losing side gets few if any. **Lord** and **Turtle** are limited by the number of spawns per game — most teams secure 1–3 turtles and 0–2 lords, with the winning team taking the majority.
:::
